description_long: This object consists of two large machete-style knives, each featuring a broad, slightly curved blade and a wooden handle with visible rivets. One blade is metallic silver with signs of wear and discoloration, while the other is heavily stained with a reddish coloration, suggesting either paint or simulated blood. The handles are brown, likely made of wood, and are attached to the blades with three metal rivets each. The blades are single-edged and taper to a point, with a utilitarian, rugged appearance suitable for heavy-duty cutting or chopping. There are no visible guards or elaborate decorations, emphasizing a functional design. The overall look is weathered and utilitarian, fitting for use in urban or outdoor environments.
description: A pair of large, weathered machete-style knives with wooden handles and broad, single-edged blades—one metallic and one stained red.
description_view_0: A direct end-on view showing the thin profile of the blades and the narrow width of the handles.
description_view_1: A side view displaying both knives, highlighting the broad, curved blades and the contrast between the metallic and red-stained finishes.
description_view_2: A reverse end-on view, again emphasizing the slimness of the blades and the alignment of the handles.
description_view_3: A mirrored side view of both knives, showing the opposite faces of the blades and handles, with the red-stained blade more prominent.
attribute_blade_type: single-edged, broad, slightly curved machete blade
attribute_handle_design: straight wooden handle with three metal rivets, no visible ergonomic shaping
